page: Microsoft Azure DC4as_cc_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DCACCV5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7763 processor, which operates at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, and the processor includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include any local storage or bundled GPUs, though it is Premium Storage capable for external disk attachments.

Performance benchmarks reveal strong single-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng single-core performance ranking in the top 25%. However, multi-core and parallelized workloads show limitations, as stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench multi-core scores, and Passmark CPU scores fall into the bottom 25%. Memory performance is mixed; large-block read-only memory bandwidth is average at 39.63 GB/sec, whereas write bandwidth and small-block cached read bandwidth are relatively weak. Single-threaded operations like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression perform at average levels, but multi-threaded decompression and database operations are weaker.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the DC4as_cc_v5 offers a dedicated hardware profile with security-oriented features like AMD SVM virtualization and cryptography extensions without the overhead of bundled local storage. This makes it a qualitatively cost-effective option for workloads that require dedicated CPU resources and moderate memory but do not demand high multi-core throughput or local disk I/O. Appropriate workloads include single-threaded applications, basic web serving, lightweight text and image processing, and general-purpose development tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure DC4as_cc_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DCACCV5 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores, 16.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance, though multi-core execution and memory write bandwidth are relatively weak. The processor supports advanced cryptography and virtualization extensions. Lacking integrated GPUs or local disks, this instance is qualitatively cost-effective for workloads relying on single-threaded performance, moderate memory capacity, and external storage. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, lightweight image and text processing, and basic web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DC4as_cc_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and no local storage, offering strong single-core performance for basic workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure DC4as_cc_v5 is a general-purpose VM featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and strong single-core benchmark performance.
tagline: A dedicated AMD-powered virtual machine offering strong single-core performance and balanced memory for general-purpose workloads without local storage.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor at 3.525 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core
Strong single-core CPU performance in benchmark testing
No bundled local storage or GPUs, supporting external Premium Storage
Suited for general-purpose, single-threaded, and light text-processing workloads
